title: TechData
description: Schema for a single tech (basic attack, support tech, or ultimate) in the Remaster Engine. v1.1.0: added 'neutral' to element enums (DEC-001/006/008), added status_id enum to status effects (DEC-002), added phase field to augmentations (DEC-007). Per §7.3 of the design document.

id.description: Lowercase snake_case identifier. Matches the filename in data/techs/.

display_name.description: Display name shown in UI. Mirrors §3.7 locked per-base tech display names.

tier.description: Magic tier slot the tech occupies (1..8). Per §3.4 / §3.8.

element.description: Color element. 7 elements per DEC-006: 6 color + 'neutral' for physical/non-elemental abilities and Chrono Cross specials.

cost_mp.description: MP cost to execute. Default 0 for basic attack line (free). Support techs and tier-8 ultimates have non-zero cost.

base_damage_multiplier.description: Multiplier applied to the base damage roll before augmentations and the resistance chart.

target_scope.description: Whom the tech targets. Closed enum matching the §7.3 TargetScope enum.

slot_kind.description: How the tech was sourced. BASIC_LINE for a base's locked basic attack. SUPPORT_FIXED for a support's locked assignment. SUPPORT_PLAYER_CHOICE for the rare open grid slot.

kind.description: What the augmentation does. Per §7.3 the six kinds are: pre/post-damage status, damage multiplier bonus, on-hit chain, MP discount, self buff.

phase.description: Per DEC-007: when in the resolution order this augmentation applies. pre = before damage step (can include MP discounts, self-buffs, status pre-applications). post = after damage step (can include on-hit chains, post-damage status). Resolver walks the list in array order; pre-phase augmentations run before the damage step, post-phase run after. A pre-phase augmentation can cancel the damage step (e.g., MP_DISCOUNT that drops cost below available MP).

chance.description: Probability the augmentation applies. Per §7.3, the §6.7 BattleRNG is the source of this roll.

status.description: Status effect to apply (when kind is *_STATUS). Per DEC-002, 8 canonical statuses: sleep, poison, burn, freeze, confuse, slow, stop, weaken.

value.description: Numeric effect of the augmentation. For DAMAGE_MULTIPLIER_BONUS this is the bonus added to base_damage_multiplier; for MP_DISCOUNT this is the MP saved; for SELF_BUFF this is the buff magnitude.

turns.description: Duration in turns for status / self-buff augmentations.

augmentations.description: List of TechAugmentation entries per §7.3 + DEC-007. Empty for the basic attack line. Resolver walks the list in array order, applying pre-phase augmentations before damage and post-phase after. Per DEC-007 idempotency rule: each (kind, status, value) triple is applied at most once per tech cast.

kind.description: What the effect does. DAMAGE for basic-attack; HEAL for restoration; APPLY_STATUS / REMOVE_STATUS for status manipulators; MODIFY_FIELD for §7.4 element grid manipulation.

magnitude.description: Magnitude of the effect. Damage/heal/buff amount.

status.description: Status effect for APPLY_STATUS / REMOVE_STATUS kinds. Per DEC-002.

effects.description: List of TechEffect entries. The basic attack line has exactly one DAMAGE effect; support techs may add heal/status/modify-field effects on top.
